revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Fest (vom 15. Tag) der Vereinigung beider Stiere'' }} Das '''Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere''' (auch '''Tag der Reinigung''') bezeichnete im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ägyptischen Mondkalender]] den Tag des [[Vollmond]]es. In den Festkalendern der [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Gau (Ägypten)|Gaue]] ist dieser Festtag landesweit als monatliches Ereignis des Vollmondes am 15. Tag des Mondkalenders vermerkt. == Hintergrund == === Astronomische Texte === [[Datei:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris gleichzeitig als Apis-Stier]] Das Vereinigungsfest ist über alle Epochen der altägyptischen Geschichte gut belegt. Als Beispiele seien hier der Text [[Esna 417]] und das [[Nutbuch]] vom Vollmondfest am 15. Tag im Mondkalender genannt: {{Zitat|Das [[Auge des Re]] ist gefüllt, das [[Horusauge|Auge des Horus]] ist geordnet. Das ist der Stier, der das Maß des Auges vollzählig machte und das ist der Stier, der richtig leuchtet am Fest des fünfzehnten Tages. [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]] und [[Osiris]] gelangen für sich zur Vereinigung der beiden Stiere im Fest. Alle Leute lassen ihre Lampen leuchten Tag und Nacht.|Auszug aus Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna.'' Kopenhagen 2000, S. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|So geschieht es, dass der [[Mond]] allein ist mit ihm (Re). Das ist das Hervorgehen des [[Horus]] gegenüber Re. Der Mond ist es im Osten, Re ist es im Westen. So entsteht die Vereinigung der beiden Stiere, der Tag der Reinigung, das Morgengrauen des Festes am fünfzehnten Tages|Auszug aus dem Nutbuch}} Die Formulierung „So geschieht es, dass der Mond allein ist mit ihm (Re)“ beschreibt sehr genau die Verhältnisse am Himmel. Zu diesem Zeitpunkt sind die [[Stern]]e und [[Planet]]en noch nicht sichtbar. Einzig [[Sonne]] und Mond sind als [[Himmelskörper]] zu sehen. === Mythologischer Hintergrund === Der Mond wird neben [[Thot]] auch mit Osiris gleichgesetzt. Der Moment des Vollmondes versinnbildlicht den starken und hitzigen Stier, während der [[Neumond]] den kastrierten Stier (sab) darstellt. Die Vereinigung von Re und Osiris wird mit dem [[Sonnenuntergang]] und dem gleichzeitigen [[Aufgang (Astronomie)#Mondaufgang und Monduntergang|Mondaufgang]] assoziiert. Sowohl [[Sonnengott|Sonnen-]] als auch [[Mondgott]] sind Symbole für die Stiere. Während der [[Mnevis|Mnevis-Stier]] als heiliges Tier für Re steht, symbolisiert der [[Apis (ägyptische Mythologie)|Apis-Stier]] die Gottheit Osiris (siehe auch [[Serapis]]). Die Feier des Vollmondtages begann am Vortag mit dem Anzünden von Fackeln, Kerzen und anderen Gegenständen. Das Festritual ist identisch mit dem [[Neith-Fest]]: {{Zitat|Entzünden von Fackeln im Inneren des Tempels. Einen schönen Tag feiern seitens der Männer und Frauen, jubeln seitens der ganzen Stadt, so dass es keinen Schlaf für irgendjemand gibt bis zum Morgengrauen.|Auszug aus [[Esna 207]]}} Dieser Sachverhalt wird auch in [[Herodot|Herodot II 62]] bestätigt, der ausdrücklich erwähnt, dass dieser Brauch auch außerhalb von [[Sais]] ausgeübt wird. Damit liefert das Fest der Vereinigung die typische Art, wie die Ägypter [[Heilig|sakral]]e Feste im privaten Rahmen hauptsächlich begingen. == Literatur == * [[Richard Anthony Parker|Richard A. Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Band 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Der Himmel über Esna. Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna'' (= ''Ägyptologische Abhandlungen.'' Band 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Zugleich: Tübingen, Univ., Magisterarbeit, 1998). * [[Kim Ryholt]] (Hrsg.): ''Hieratic texts from the collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Band 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Band 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Kopenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne. Das sogenannte Nutbuch'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Band 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Band 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies u. a., Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Einzelnachweise == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Ägyptischer Kalender (Altertum)]] [[Kategorie:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Kategorie:Astronomie im Alten Ägypten]]

Copying from [[Category:Egyptian festival pages to be written]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
/* Overview */
revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417}} and the {{ill|Nutbook|de|Nutbuch|ja|ナットブック|zh|Nutbook}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Re|de|Auge des Re|ja|ラーの眼|zh|拉的眼睛}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Re (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉}} and {{ill|Osiris|de|Osiris|ja|オシリス|zh|奥西里斯}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:両牛の合同祭]][[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] ==References== [[Category:Egyptian festival pages to be written]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:両牛の合同祭]][[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] ==References== [[Category:Egyptian festival pages to be written]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417}} and the {{ill|Nutbook|de|Nutbuch|ja|ナットブック|zh|Nutbook}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Re|de|Auge des Re|ja|ラーの眼|zh|拉的眼睛}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Re (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉}} and {{ill|Osiris|de|Osiris|ja|オシリス|zh|奥西里斯}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:両牛の合同祭]][[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:両牛の合同祭]][[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417}} and the {{ill|Nutbook|de|Nutbuch|ja|ナットブック|zh|Nutbook}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Re|de|Auge des Re|ja|ラーの眼|zh|拉的眼睛}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Re (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉}} and {{ill|Osiris|de|Osiris|ja|オシリス|zh|奥西里斯}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:両牛の合同祭]][[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特}}. '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417}} and the {{ill|Nutbook|de|Nutbuch|ja|ナットブック|zh|Nutbook}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Re|de|Auge des Re|ja|ラーの眼|zh|拉的眼睛}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Re (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉}} and {{ill|Osiris|de|Osiris|ja|オシリス|zh|奥西里斯}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:両牛の合同祭]][[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417}} and the {{ill|Nutbook|de|Nutbuch|ja|ナットブック|zh|Nutbook}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Re|de|Auge des Re|ja|ラーの眼|zh|拉的眼睛}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Re (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉}} and {{ill|Osiris|de|Osiris|ja|オシリス|zh|奥西里斯}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:両牛の合同祭]][[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:両牛の合同祭]][[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] ==References== [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417}} and the {{ill|Nutbook|de|Nutbuch|ja|ナットブック|zh|Nutbook}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Re|de|Auge des Re|ja|ラーの眼|zh|拉的眼睛}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Re (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉}} and {{ill|Osiris|de|Osiris|ja|オシリス|zh|奥西里斯}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:両牛の合同祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]]

Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt|12=draft}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:両牛の合同祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]]

revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历|12=draft}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月|12=draft}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|12=draft}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美|12=draft}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Nutbook|de|Nutbuch|ja|ナットブック|zh|Nutbook|12=draft}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Re|de|Auge des Re|ja|ラーの眼|zh|拉的眼睛|12=draft}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Re (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Osiris|de|Osiris|ja|オシリス|zh|奥西里斯|12=draft}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星|12=draft}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星|12=draft}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳|12=draft}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特|12=draft}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落|12=draft}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出|12=draft}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神|12=draft}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛|12=draft}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛|12=draft}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯|12=draft}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节|12=draft}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207|12=draft}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62|12=draft}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯|12=draft}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt|12=draft}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:両牛の合同祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]]

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:両牛の合同祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]]

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历|12=draft|12=draft}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Nutbook|de|Nutbuch|ja|ナットブック|zh|Nutbook|12=draft|12=draft}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Re|de|Auge des Re|ja|ラーの眼|zh|拉的眼睛|12=draft|12=draft}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft|12=draft}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Re (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Osiris|de|Osiris|ja|オシリス|zh|奥西里斯|12=draft|12=draft}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft|12=draft}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft|12=draft}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星|12=draft|12=draft}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特|12=draft|12=draft}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft|12=draft}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落|12=draft|12=draft}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt=Sun-|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神|12=draft|12=draft}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛|12=draft|12=draft}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛|12=draft|12=draft}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯|12=draft|12=draft}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207|12=draft|12=draft}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62|12=draft|12=draft}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯|12=draft|12=draft}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt|12=draft|12=draft}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:両牛の合同祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]]

revision.text:
'''Unification Festival of the Two Bulls''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历|12=draft|12=draft}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Nutbook|de|Nutbuch|ja|ナットブック|zh|Nutbook|12=draft|12=draft}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Re|de|Auge des Re|ja|ラーの眼|zh|拉的眼睛|12=draft|12=draft}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft|12=draft}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Re (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|ja|ラー|zh|拉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Osiris|de|Osiris|ja|オシリス|zh|奥西里斯|12=draft|12=draft}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft|12=draft}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft|12=draft}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|stars|de|Stern|ja|星|zh|星|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|planets|de|Planet|ja|惑星|zh|行星|12=draft|12=draft}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|de|Sonne|ja|太陽|zh|太阳|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特|12=draft|12=draft}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft|12=draft}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|ja|日の入り|zh|日落|12=draft|12=draft}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both the {{ill|Sun god|de|Sonnengott|ja|太陽神|zh|太阳神|lt_ja=太陽-|lt_de=Sonnen-|lt_zh=太阳-|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神|12=draft|12=draft}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛|12=draft|12=draft}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛|12=draft|12=draft}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|de|Serapis|ja|セラピス|zh|塞拉皮斯|12=draft|12=draft}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207|12=draft|12=draft}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|lt=Herodotus II 62|lt_ja=ヘロドトス II 62|lt_de=Herodot II 62|lt_zh=希罗多德 II 62|12=draft|12=draft}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|サイス|zh|塞斯|12=draft|12=draft}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard A. Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|zh|Richard A. Parker|lt=Richard A. Parker|lt_ja=リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|lt_de=Richard A. Parker|lt_zh=Richard A. Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|de|Kim Ryholt|ja|キム・リヨルト|zh|Kim Ryholt|12=draft|12=draft}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navigation}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar]] [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:両牛の合同祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[sn:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[en:Unification Festival of the Two Bulls]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|12=draft]]

''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历|12=draft|12=draft}} the day of the {{ill|Full Moon|de|Vollmond|ja|満月|zh|满月|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|altägyptischen|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history.
