revision.text:
[[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] '''Unification Festival of the Two Bulls''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian lunar calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the day of the {{ill|Full moon|en|Full moon|WD=Q104641|lt=Full Moon}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nomes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 417|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Nutbook}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|WD=Q760218|lt=Eye of Re}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eye of Horus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホルスの目|1=Eye of Horus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q211286}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian mythology)}} and {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|en|Moon|WD=Q405|lt=Moon}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=stars}} and {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|WD=Q634|lt=planets}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|en|Sun|WD=Q525|lt=Sun}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|en|Thoth|WD=Q146921|lt=Thoth}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New moon|en|New moon|WD=Q108566|lt=New Moon}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|Sunset|en|Sunset|WD=Q166564|lt=sunset}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moonrise|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天体の出没|1=moonrise|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692543}}. Both the {{ill|Solar deity|en|Solar deity|WD=Q511056|lt=Sun god}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moon god|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mnevis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apis bull|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|en|Serapis|WD=Q214554|lt=Serapis}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neith Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|en|Herodotus|WD=Q26825|lt=Herodotus II 62}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais, Egypt|en|Sais, Egypt|WD=Q381038|lt=Sais}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard A. Parker|en|Richard A. Parker|WD=Q7323591|lt=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|en|Kim Ryholt|WD=Q443108|lt=Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|version=237094396|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:両牛の合同祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q2515142}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|version=237094396|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:両牛の合同祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q2515142}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

[[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] '''Unification Festival of the Two Bulls''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Sensen-Kawi |NAME = W3:N13 s:n-s:n E1:w-M17 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Sensen-Kawi
''snsn-k3.wj'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Festival (of the 15th Day) of the Union of the Two Bulls'' }} The '''Festival of the Union of the Two Bulls''' (also '''Day of Purification''') designated in the {{ill|Egyptian lunar calendar|de|ägyptischen Mondkalender|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|埃及阴历|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian lunar calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the day of the {{ill|Full moon|en|Full moon|WD=Q104641|lt=Full Moon|1=Full moon}}. In the festival calendars of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|nomes|de|Gaue|ja|ノメ|zh|诺美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nomes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this festival day is recorded nationwide as a monthly event on the 15th day of the lunar calendar. == Background == === Astronomical Texts === [[File:Osiris-Apis MGEg Inv22847-9.jpg|thumb|Osiris simultaneously as the Apis Bull]] The union festival is well documented throughout all periods of Ancient Egyptian history. Examples include the text {{ill|Esna 417|de|Esna 417|ja|エスナ417|zh|Esna 417|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 417|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Nutbook|1=Book of Nut}} of the full moon festival on the 15th day of the lunar calendar: {{Zitat|The {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|WD=Q760218|lt=Eye of Re|1=Eye of Ra}} is filled, the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Auge des Horus|ja|ホルスの眼|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eye of Horus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホルスの目|1=Eye of Horus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q211286}} is arranged. This is the bull that perfectly measured the eye, and this is the bull that shines rightly at the festival of the fifteenth day. {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian mythology)|1=Ra}} and {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}} proceed to unite the two bulls in the festival. All people let their lamps shine day and night.|Excerpt from Esna 417Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Copenhagen 2000, pp. 85–87.}} {{Zitat|Thus it happens that the {{ill|Moon|en|Moon|WD=Q405|lt=Moon|1=Moon}} is alone with him (Re). This is the emergence of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}} in relation to Re. The Moon is in the east, Re is in the west. In this way, the union of the two bulls is formed—the Day of Purification, the dawn of the festival on the fifteenth day.|Excerpt from the Nutbook}} The formulation “Thus it happens that the Moon is alone with him (Re)” very accurately describes the conditions in the sky. At this time, the {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=stars|1=Star}} and {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|WD=Q634|lt=planets|1=Planet}} are not yet visible. Only the {{ill|Sun|en|Sun|WD=Q525|lt=Sun|1=Sun}} and the Moon appear as celestial bodies. === Mythological Background === The Moon is equated with Osiris alongside {{ill|Thoth|en|Thoth|WD=Q146921|lt=Thoth|1=Thoth}}. The moment of the full moon symbolizes the strong and fiery bull, while the {{ill|New moon|en|New moon|WD=Q108566|lt=New Moon|1=New moon}} represents the castrated bull (sab). The union of Re and Osiris is associated with the {{ill|Sunset|en|Sunset|WD=Q166564|lt=sunset|1=Sunset}} and the simultaneous {{ill|moonrise|de|Mondaufgang|ja|月の出|zh|月出|lt=moonrise|lt_ja=月の出|lt_de=Mondaufgang|lt_zh=月出|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moonrise|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天体の出没|1=moonrise|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692543}}. Both the {{ill|Solar deity|en|Solar deity|WD=Q511056|lt=Sun god|1=Solar deity}} and the {{ill|Moon god|de|Mondgott|ja|月の神|zh|月神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moon god|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} serve as symbols of the bulls. While the {{ill|Mnevis|de|Mnevis-Stier|ja|ムネヴィス|zh|Mnevis公牛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mnevis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands as the sacred animal for Re, the {{ill|Apis bull|de|Apis-Stier|ja|アピスの雄牛|zh|阿皮斯公牛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apis bull|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} symbolizes the deity Osiris (see also {{ill|Serapis|en|Serapis|WD=Q214554|lt=Serapis|1=Serapis}}). The celebration of the full moon day began on the preceding day with the lighting of torches, candles, and other objects. The festival ritual is identical to the {{ill|Neith Festival|de|Neith-Fest|ja|ネイス祭|zh|尼特节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neith Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: {{Zitat|Lighting torches inside the temple. A beautiful day is celebrated by both men and women, with the entire city rejoicing, so that no one sleeps until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|Esna 207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} This custom is also confirmed in {{ill|Herodotus|en|Herodotus|WD=Q26825|lt=Herodotus II 62|1=Herodotus}}, who explicitly mentions that this practice was also observed outside of {{ill|Sais, Egypt|en|Sais, Egypt|WD=Q381038|lt=Sais|1=Sais, Egypt}}. Thus, the union festival exemplifies the typical manner in which the Egyptians celebrated sacred festivals privately. == Literature == * {{ill|Richard A. Parker|en|Richard A. Parker|WD=Q7323591|lt=Richard A. Parker|1=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491464|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz and others, 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study of Religious Astronomy in Egypt Based on the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises.'' Vol. 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Master’s thesis, University of Tübingen, 1998). * {{ill|Kim Ryholt|en|Kim Ryholt|WD=Q443108|lt=Kim Ryholt|1=Kim Ryholt}} (ed.): ''Hieratic Texts from the Collection'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 7 = ''CNI Publications.'' Vol. 30). Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars. The So-called Nutbook'' (= ''The Carlsberg Papyri.'' Vol. 8 = ''CNI Publications'' Vol. 31). The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen and others, 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|version=237094396|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:両牛の合同祭]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere]] [[de:Vereinigungsfest der beiden Stiere|qq=draft]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q2515142}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]
